system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling.Your task is to generate a complete and functional file named: <file_name>blockMeshDict</file_name> within the <folder_name>system</folder_name> directory. Ensure all required values are present and match with the files content already generated.Before finalizing the output, ensure:
- All necessary fields exist (e.g., if `nu` is defined in `constant/transportProperties`, it must be used correctly in `0/U`).
- Cross-check field names between different files to avoid mismatches.
- Ensure units and dimensions are correct** for all physical variables.
- Ensure case solver settings are consistent with the user's requirements. Available solvers are: ['compressibleInterFoam', 'cavitatingFoam', 'SRFSimpleFoam', 'solidEquilibriumDisplacementFoam', 'pimpleFoam', 'compressibleMultiphaseInterFoam', 'PDRFoam', 'reactingFoam', 'dsmcFoam', 'XiFoam', 'potentialFreeSurfaceFoam', 'foamyQuadMesh', 'chemFoam', 'electrostaticFoam', 'foamyHexMesh', 'simpleFoam', 'mdFoam', 'interFoam', 'potentialFoam', 'financialFoam', 'rhoPorousSimpleFoam', 'buoyantReactingFoam', 'rhoSimpleFoam', 'multiphaseEulerFoam', 'laplacianFoam', 'interMixingFoam', 'twoLiquidMixingFoam', 'boundaryFoam', 'pisoFoam', 'particleFoam', 'blockMesh', 'chtMultiRegionFoam', 'buoyantFoam', 'icoFoam', 'rhoPimpleFoam', 'mdEquilibrationFoam', 'rhoCentralFoam', 'porousSimpleFoam', 'solidDisplacementFoam', 'scalarTransportFoam', 'dnsFoam', 'refineMesh', 'SRFPimpleFoam', 'shallowWaterFoam', 'driftFluxFoam', 'adjointShapeOptimisationFoam', 'multiphaseInterFoam', 'mhdFoam', 'denseParticleFoam', 'moveDynamicMesh', 'snappyHexMesh'].
Provide only the code—no explanations, comments, or additional text.
user_prompt:
User requirement: Perform a 3D Bernard Cell simulation using OpenFOAM buoyantFoam solver. The computational domain spans 9 m x 1 m x 2 m. The simulation begins at t=0 seconds and runs until t = 1000 seconds with a time step of 1 second, and results are written at intervals of every 50 seconds. One wall has a temperature of 305 K, while the other has a temperature of 300 K.
Refer to the following similar case file content if helpful:
<similar_case_reference><index>
case name: BernardCells
case domain: heatTransfer
case category: None
case solver: buoyantFoam
</index>
<directory_structure>
<dir>directory name: 0. File names in this directory: [T, U, alphat, epsilon, k, nut, p, p_rgh]</dir>
<dir>directory name: system. File names in this directory: [blockMeshDict, controlDict, fvSchemes, fvSolution]</dir>
<dir>directory name: constant. File names in this directory: [g, momentumTransport, physicalProperties]</dir>
</directory_structure></similar_case_reference>
match_level='high' use_scope='All files from the reference BernardCells case (system/controlDict, system/fvSchemes, system/fvSolution, constant/thermophysicalProperties, constant/transportProperties, constant/turbulenceProperties, constant/g, 0/U, 0/T, 0/p) can be used as direct templates; modify blockMeshDict for the 9×1×2 m domain, controlDict for endTime=1000s, deltaT=1s, writeInterval=50, and boundary conditions for T (305 K and 300 K walls).' advice='Use the matched BernardCells tutorial case as a full template since it matches exactly in case type, solver, and physics—adopt its directory structure, schemes, schemes, thermophysical properties, mesh, and boundary condition layout directly, then adjust the geometry dimensions (blockMeshDict), time controls (controlDict), and thermal boundary values (0/T) to the specific requirements provided by the user.'If the similar case is a weak match, do not copy it blindly. Use it only where it is consistent with the user requirement. Just modify the necessary parts to make the file complete and functional.Please ensure that the generated file is complete, functional, and logically sound.Additionally, apply your domain expertise to verify that all numerical values are consistent with the user's requirements, maintaining accuracy and coherence.When generating controlDict, do not include anything to preform post processing. Just include the necessary settings to run the simulation.
